instruction: Rewrite the description in clear, professional cybersecurity language.
input: In Open-iSCSI tcmu-runner 1.3.x, 1.4.x, and 1.5.x through 1.5.2, xcopy_locate_udev in tcmur_cmd_handler.c lacks a check for transport-layer restrictions, allowing remote attackers to read or write files via directory traversal in an XCOPY request. For example, an attack can occur over a network if the attacker has access to one iSCSI LUN. NOTE: relative to CVE-2020-28374, this is a similar mistake in a different algorithm.
output: A vulnerability exists in Open-iSCSI tcmu-runner versions 1.3.x, 1.4.x, and 1.5.x up to 1.5.2, where the xcopy_locate_udev function in the tcmur_cmd_handler.c file fails to enforce transport-layer restrictions. This flaw enables remote attackers to perform directory traversal attacks, allowing unauthorized file read or write operations via an XCOPY request. This issue can be exploited over a network when an attacker has access to an iSCSI Logical Unit Number (LUN). This vulnerability bears similarity to CVE-2020-28374 but occurs in a different algorithm.
